project.name: Atlas CRM Migration
project.description: Migrate Contoso Financial's legacy CRM to Dynamics 365 Sales, with NorthPeak Systems delivering configuration + core-banking integration and Veridian Consulting delivering the data migration.

milestones.title: Dev & Test environments provisioned
milestones.notes: Dynamics sandboxes stood up by NorthPeak.

milestones.title: BRD sign-off
milestones.notes: Business Requirements Document v1.0 approved by Contoso.

milestones.title: Config sprint 1 — accounts & contacts
milestones.notes: Slipped: dependent on remediated data and API sandbox access.

milestones.title: Core-banking API integration
milestones.notes: Nightly batch pattern; blocked-adjacent on sandbox credentials.

milestones.title: UAT begins
milestones.notes: Shifted later to absorb the config sprint 1 slip.

milestones.title: End-user training
milestones.notes: Not yet scheduled — pending confirmed UAT and go-live dates.

actions.title: Draft UAT test plan and entry criteria
Circulate revised project plan to steering committee

decisions.summary: Split delivery — NorthPeak owns configuration & integration, Veridian owns data migration.
decisions.rationale: Plays to each vendor's strength and isolates the data-migration risk under a specialist.

decisions.summary: Target platform confirmed as Microsoft Dynamics 365 Sales.
decisions.rationale: Aligns with Contoso's existing M365 tenant and licensing.

decisions.summary: Integrate to the core-banking system via nightly batch API rather than real-time.
decisions.rationale: Real-time was disproportionate to need and raised the security-review burden.

decisions.summary: Defer advanced analytics & reporting to phase 2.
decisions.rationale: Protects the go-live date; core pipeline reporting is sufficient for launch.

decisions.summary: Adopt a phased regional cutover instead of a single big-bang cutover.
decisions.rationale: Reduces go-live blast radius given unresolved data-quality issues.

decisions.summary: Shift UAT start to 8 September to absorb the config sprint 1 slip.
decisions.rationale: Preserves a realistic UAT window rather than compressing testing.

risks.title: Legacy data quality far worse than assumed (duplicates, missing fields)
risks.mitigation: Dedicated remediation sprint; Veridian dedup tooling; profiling report driving cleanup.

risks.title: Core-banking API sandbox access delayed by Contoso security review
risks.mitigation: Escalated to sponsor; provisional mock endpoint for early build.

risks.title: NorthPeak configuration team under-staffed for sprint 2
risks.mitigation: Additional configurator being onboarded for August.

risks.title: Compressed UAT window threatens go-live quality
risks.mitigation: UAT shifted to 8 Sep; entry criteria being defined up front.

risks.title: Reporting requirements expanding beyond phase 1 scope
risks.mitigation: Advanced analytics formally deferred to phase 2.

issues.title: 12,400 duplicate customer records in the legacy extract
issues.mitigation: Dedup rules defined; remediation in progress under Veridian.

issues.title: Test-environment latency during large batch loads
issues.mitigation: NorthPeak retuned the batch window; acceptable in retest.

dependencies.title: Integration build blocked on core-banking API sandbox credentials
Migration dry run depends on the remediated data extract

requirements.title: Relationship manager manages a sales opportunity through the pipeline
requirements.detail: RM progresses opportunities through stage-based pipeline stages, with a weighted forecast rolled up for their book of business.

scenarios.title: Advancing an opportunity updates the forecast
given: a relationship manager has an open opportunity at the 'Proposal' stage
then: the opportunity's stage and probability update
the forecast for the relationship manager's book is recalculated

scenarios.title: Pipeline view shows a weighted forecast for the book
given: a relationship manager has several opportunities across pipeline stages
then: opportunities are grouped by stage
a weighted forecast total is shown for the book

requirements.title: Role-based access control aligned to Contoso security policy
requirements.detail: MFA for all users; least-privilege roles mapped to Contoso's IAM standard.

requirements.title: Account view loads in under 3 seconds at 50k accounts
requirements.detail: Performance target for the primary relationship-manager view.

requirements.title: Relationship manager onboards a new corporate customer
requirements.detail: RM captures the customer, links banking products, and creates an onboarding opportunity.

scenarios.title: Onboard a new corporate customer with product linkage
given: a relationship manager is signed in
the customer does not yet exist in the CRM
when: they create a new corporate customer
they link the customer's banking products
then: an onboarding opportunity is created
a KYC review task is assigned to the RM
the customer appears in the RM's book of business

scenarios.title: Prospect already holds a mortgage in core banking
given: a relationship manager is signed in
the prospect already holds a mortgage in core banking
then: the existing mortgage is surfaced automatically
the RM is prompted to confirm the linkage

requirements.title: Migrate a relationship manager's existing book of business
requirements.detail: Bulk-migrate an RM's accounts, contacts and open opportunities with data lineage preserved.

openQuestions.question: What is the data-retention policy for migrated closed accounts?
Is MFA required for all CRM users or only privileged roles?
openQuestions.answer: All users — confirmed at steering committee on 8 July.

openQuestions.question: What production cutover downtime window is acceptable to the business?
What approval threshold applies to bulk reassignments above 500 accounts?

What weekly-active-usage or preference target should the RM-adoption OKR key result be held to? It currently has no baseline, target or unit — the aspirational objective can't be scored until we quantify it.

artifacts.oneLiner: Project kickoff — scope, vendor split, teams and initial timeline agreed.
Business Requirements Document v1.0 — functional, non-functional and use-case requirements.
Solution design — environments, integration pattern and data model.
Weekly status — environments live, profiling underway, sprint 1 starting.
Steering committee #1 — profiling complete, risks reviewed, analytics deferred.
Veridian data-migration plan & runbook — dry-run approach and remediation.
Weekly status — sprint 1 slipped, dry run at risk, data quality escalated.
Steering committee #2 — UAT moved to 8 Sep, phased cutover adopted, MFA scope confirmed.
Imported BDD/Gherkin spec — agreed acceptance scenarios for account view, batch sync and onboarding.

narrative.iWant: a single 360 view of a customer across all their Atlas accounts
narrative.soThat: I can advise them without switching between the core-banking screens

rules.title: All of a customer's linked accounts appear on one screen

examples.title: Retail + mortgage in one household
examples.context: Household H-4471 holds a Contoso retail current account and a mortgage
examples.action: An RM opens the customer record
examples.outcome: Both accounts are listed together under the household

examples.title: Joint account holders
examples.context: A joint savings account is held by Priya and Sam Okafor
examples.outcome: Both holders' names are shown against the joint account

examples.title: Recently closed account
examples.context: A current account was closed 3 months ago
examples.outcome: The closed account is shown greyed with a 'Closed' badge, not hidden

examples.title: Accounts, products held and recent interactions together
examples.context: Household H-4471 holds a current account and a credit card, with several branch and call-centre interactions in the last quarter
examples.action: An RM opens the customer record
examples.outcome: Accounts, the products held and interactions from the last 90 days are shown together on one screen

examples.title: Customer with no linked banking products
examples.context: A newly created customer has no linked banking products yet
examples.action: An RM opens the customer record
examples.outcome: The products panel shows a friendly empty-state message rather than an error or blank space

rules.title: Balances reflect core-banking within the sync SLA and never show stale data silently

examples.title: Balances after the nightly batch
examples.context: The nightly batch completed at 02:00
examples.action: An RM opens the customer at 08:00
examples.outcome: Balances show an 'as of 02:00' timestamp

examples.title: Intraday transfer not yet synced
examples.context: A GBP 5,000 transfer posted at 10:00 has not synced
examples.action: An RM opens the customer at 10:05
examples.outcome: The last-sync time is shown so the RM knows the figure may lag

examples.title: Batch failed overnight
examples.context: The 02:00 batch failed and did not complete
examples.outcome: A banner warns 'Balances may be delayed' instead of showing yesterday's data as current

examples.title: Product holdings reconcile overnight, not just balances
examples.context: A customer took out a new credit card yesterday in core banking
examples.action: An RM opens the customer after the nightly sync
examples.outcome: The new product holding appears alongside balances, reconciled from core banking

rules.title: An RM only sees customers and data they are authorised for

examples.title: Customer in the RM's book
examples.context: RM Meera is assigned to household H-4471
examples.outcome: Access is allowed and the full 360 view loads

examples.title: Customer outside the RM's book
examples.context: A customer is assigned to a different RM's book
examples.action: Meera attempts to open that customer
examples.outcome: Access is blocked with a 'not authorised' message

examples.title: Private-banking flagged customer
examples.context: A customer is flagged private-banking and the RM lacks clearance
examples.outcome: Sensitive fields are masked while basic contact details remain

scenarios.title: Linked accounts appear together for a household
given: a household holds a retail current account and a mortgage
when: a relationship manager opens the customer record
then: both accounts appear together on one screen

scenarios.title: Balances are stamped with their sync time
given: the nightly balance batch completed at 02:00
when: a relationship manager opens the customer at 08:00
then: each balance shows an 'as of 02:00' timestamp

scenarios.title: Access outside the assigned book is blocked
given: a relationship manager is not assigned to a customer's book
when: the relationship manager attempts to open that customer
then: access is blocked with a not-authorised message

scenarios.title: Accounts, products and recent interactions appear together
given: a household holds a current account and a credit card
the customer has branch and call-centre interactions in the last 90 days
when: a relationship manager opens the customer record
then: accounts and products held appear together on one screen
interactions from the last 90 days are listed

scenarios.title: Products panel shows an empty state when nothing is linked
given: a customer has no linked banking products
when: a relationship manager opens the customer record
then: the products panel shows an empty-state message instead of an error

scenarios.title: A failed overnight batch warns instead of showing stale data
given: the overnight balance batch failed and did not complete
when: a relationship manager opens the customer
then: a banner warns that balances may be delayed
yesterday's balances are not shown as if they were current

userStories.title: Bulk-reassign a departing RM's book of business

narrative.iWant: to reassign a departing relationship manager's accounts in bulk
narrative.soThat: customer coverage continues without manual one-by-one edits

rules.title: A whole book can be moved to another RM in a single action

examples.title: Move an entire book
examples.context: RM Tom Wolfe is leaving with 180 assigned accounts
examples.action: Ops selects Tom's book and reassigns it to RM Ana Garcia
examples.outcome: All 180 accounts show Ana as the owning RM

examples.title: Reassignment is auditable
examples.context: A bulk reassignment of 180 accounts is performed
examples.outcome: An audit entry records who reassigned what, when, and from/to whom

examples.title: Move a selected subset of a book
examples.context: Operations needs to move only the SME accounts from a departing RM's book, not the retail ones
examples.action: Ops filters the book to SME accounts and reassigns just those to another RM
examples.outcome: Only the selected SME accounts move; the rest stay with the original RM

rules.title: Bulk reassignment requires the right permission, and large moves need manager approval

examples.title: Ops lead has reassignment rights
examples.context: A sales-operations lead holds the bulk-reassign permission
examples.outcome: The reassignment proceeds without extra approval

examples.title: User without permission is blocked
examples.context: A relationship manager without bulk-reassign rights
examples.outcome: The action is blocked with a 'not authorised' message

examples.title: Large reassignment needs manager sign-off
examples.context: A reassignment covers 180 accounts, above the 100-account approval threshold
examples.outcome: It is held pending a sales manager's sign-off before taking effect

rules.title: Customers keep continuous coverage and are notified appropriately when their RM changes

examples.title: Customer notified of their new RM
examples.context: 180 accounts move to a new relationship manager
examples.outcome: Affected customers receive a notification introducing their new relationship manager

examples.title: Open opportunities and tasks follow the accounts
examples.context: The departing RM had open opportunities and tasks on the reassigned accounts
examples.action: The book is reassigned to another RM
examples.outcome: The open opportunities and tasks move to the new RM so nothing is dropped

examples.title: Do-not-contact customers are respected
examples.context: Some reassigned customers are flagged do-not-contact for marketing
examples.outcome: They still receive the mandatory relationship-manager-change notice but no marketing content

scenarios.title: A whole book moves to another RM in one action
given: a departing relationship manager has 180 assigned accounts
when: operations reassigns the whole book to another relationship manager in one action
then: all 180 accounts show the new relationship manager as owner

scenarios.title: A reassignment above the threshold waits for manager approval
then: the reassignment is held pending a sales manager's approval
it takes effect only once approved

scenarios.title: Open work follows the accounts to the new RM
given: a departing relationship manager has open opportunities and tasks on the reassigned accounts
when: operations reassigns the book to another relationship manager
then: the open opportunities and tasks are reassigned to the new relationship manager
no open work is left unassigned

okrs.objective: Reach a trustworthy, UAT-ready Atlas CRM foundation by the end of Q3 2026

keyResults.statement: Reduce duplicate customer records to under 400 before the migration dry run
Complete both CRM configuration sprints on the revised plan
Cut the relationship-manager account view to under 3 seconds at 50k accounts

okrs.objective: Make Atlas the CRM relationship managers genuinely prefer over the legacy system

keyResults.statement: A clear majority of relationship managers rate Atlas easier to use than the legacy CRM
Relationship managers complete Atlas onboarding training ahead of go-live
